hero.eyebrow: 80 C R290 Full DC Inverter Series
hero.title: High-temperature heating, engineered around efficiency.
hero.body: Air-to-water heat-pump systems for heating, cooling and hot water, with selected R290 models delivering outlet water up to 80 C.
hero.imageAlt: GOLD high-temperature air-to-water heat pump in a premium technical setting

proof.label: Heating capacity range
proof.note: Multiple capacities for different project needs

proof.label: R290 refrigerant
proof.note: Natural refrigerant with low climate impact

systemIntro.title: Move heat into your building instead of creating it from fuel.
systemIntro.body: The heat pump captures energy from outdoor air and transfers it to the water circuit. Full-inverter control continuously adjusts the compressor, fan and circulation package to match demand.
steps: Raise its temperature through the refrigerant circuit
Transfer heat to the building water system
Modulate output as operating conditions change

benefits.title: Designed as a complete heating and cooling platform.

items.title: High-temperature capability
items.body: Selected R290 models provide outlet water up to 80 C for demanding hydronic applications.

items.title: Full-inverter control
items.body: Variable-speed components adapt output to system demand for smoother, more efficient operation.

items.title: Integrated circulation package
items.body: The current 80 C line includes an inverter circulation pump and expansion tank inside the unit.

items.title: Lower-impact refrigerant
items.body: The R290 series uses a refrigerant listed with a global-warming potential of 3.

technical.title: 80 C R290 Full DC Inverter Heat Pump
technical.body: A high-temperature platform combining a Panasonic full-inverter compressor, Danfoss plate heat exchanger and EEV, smart control and an integrated circulation package. Capacity is selected around each project's operating needs.
items: Danfoss plate heat exchanger and EEV

smartControl.title: Clear control, right where you need it.
smartControl.body: LCD control and Wi-Fi access make it easier to set operating modes, adjust temperatures and follow system status.

aboutTeaser.title: Heating and cooling experience in Syria since 2008.
aboutTeaser.body: From product selection to system planning and installation support, Gold Group works to turn technical equipment into a coordinated, practical solution.

productsTeaser.title: The components that complete the system.
productsTeaser.body: Hot-water cylinders, buffer tanks, expansion tanks and circulation pumps selected around the needs of the complete installation.

solutions.title: Start with the application, then select the equipment.
solutions.body: Explore how heat pumps and hydronic underfloor heating fit into a complete building system before choosing capacities and components.

cta.title: Planning a heating or hot-water project?
cta.body: Share the building type, area and required water temperature. Our team will help identify the right system direction.

about.title: Local project knowledge, connected to modern heating technology.
about.intro: Gold Group has worked in heating and cooling in Syria since 2008. The company focuses on heat-pump systems and the supporting equipment needed to deliver reliable comfort and hot water.
about.story: Our role is not limited to supplying a machine. We help connect capacity, water temperature, building distribution, circulation and storage into one coherent system direction, then coordinate the selected solution with project requirements.

values.title: System thinking
values.body: We look at the complete water and air-side system, not only the headline capacity of one unit.

values.title: Clear selection
values.body: Specifications and project conditions should be understandable before equipment is ordered.

values.title: Efficiency with context
values.body: Performance claims are tied to models, temperatures and stated test conditions.

values.title: Long-term support
values.body: Product supply is supported by installation coordination, commissioning guidance and after-sales communication.

products.title: System quality starts with every component.
products.intro: At Gold Group, system performance is not built by one device alone. We select the heat pump, storage, circulation, pipework and accessories as one coordinated system matched to the project.
products.imageAlt: GOLD hot-water cylinder, buffer tank, expansion tank and circulation pump

categories.name: Hot-water cylinders
categories.summary: Store and deliver domestic hot water while working with the heat source and the building's demand profile.

categories.name: Buffer tanks
categories.summary: Add thermal volume and hydraulic stability to help the heat-pump system operate smoothly.

categories.name: Expansion tanks
categories.summary: Manage water-volume change as temperature varies and help maintain safe system pressure.

categories.name: Circulation pumps
categories.summary: Move water through heating, cooling and domestic hot-water circuits at the required flow and head.

heatPumps.title: A heat-pump system shaped around the building, water circuit and climate.
heatPumps.intro: An air-to-water heat pump transfers energy from outdoor air into a building's water system. Correct selection connects the unit with the real heating and cooling loads, required water temperatures, emitters, circulation and controls.

overview.title: One platform for heating, cooling and hot water.
overview.body: Depending on the selected model and system design, a heat pump can supply space heating, chilled or cooled water, and domestic hot-water storage. The hydraulic circuit and controls determine how these applications operate together.

applications.title: Space heating
applications.body: Supplies compatible hydronic distribution such as underfloor circuits, fan-coil units and other water-side terminal equipment designed for the required supply temperature.

applications.title: Cooling
applications.body: Selected reversible systems can supply cooled water to suitable terminal units and a correctly designed condensate strategy.

applications.title: Domestic hot water
applications.body: Works with a correctly sized hot-water cylinder, heat exchanger arrangement and control sequence.

energyManagement.title: Power that follows demand, not fixed maximum-output operation.
energyManagement.body: Full-inverter control raises output when heating demand is high, then progressively reduces compressor, fan and circulation speed as the system approaches its target temperature. Electrical input therefore varies within each model's operating range according to building load, outdoor conditions and required supply-water temperature instead of remaining fixed at one output level.
energyManagement.closing: The result is smarter control of electrical input for the useful heat transferred into the building.

steps.title: Respond to demand
steps.body: Output rises during start-up or when the thermal load increases.

steps.title: Approach the target
steps.body: Component speed reduces progressively as system temperature stabilizes.

steps.title: Maintain balance
steps.body: The system modulates within the range required by real operating conditions.

heatPumps.seriesBody: The current GOLD range includes two air-to-water platforms. Exact performance depends on the selected model and operating conditions.

series.title: R290 high-temperature series
series.body: Full-inverter models in 8, 12, 16 and 22 kW heating capacities, with outlet water up to 80 C.

series.title: R32 full-inverter series
series.body: Models in 8, 12.5, 16 and 22 kW heating capacities, with outlet water up to 60 C and cabinet configuration varying by capacity.

heatPumps.factorsTitle: Inputs used to frame the right selection
heatPumps.factorsIntro: Capacity alone is not enough. A useful project review starts from the building and the operating conditions.

factors.title: Design heat load
factors.body: The building's calculated demand at local winter design conditions.

factors.title: Climate
factors.body: Outdoor design temperatures and the seasonal conditions in the project location.

factors.title: Required water temperature
factors.body: The supply temperature needed by the selected emitters and domestic hot-water strategy.

factors.title: Hydraulic design
factors.body: Flow rates, pressure loss, buffer volume, separation and circulation requirements.

factors.title: Heat-distribution equipment
factors.body: Underfloor circuits, fan-coil units and compatible water-side terminal equipment selected for the system operating temperatures.

factors.title: Controls
factors.body: Zones, sensors, operating priorities and the coordination of heating, cooling and hot water.

heatPumps.closingTitle: The product and the water system must be selected together.
heatPumps.closingBody: Gold Group can review the building type, area, location, required temperatures and distribution method to help define the appropriate next technical step.

underfloorHeating.title: Low-temperature comfort begins below the finished floor.
underfloorHeating.intro: Hydronic underfloor heating circulates warm water through pipe loops embedded within the floor construction. The large active surface can distribute heat evenly when the building load, insulation, layout, flow and controls are designed as one system.

overview.title: A water-based heating system, not only a pipe layout.
overview.body: The floor build-up becomes the heat emitter. Pipe spacing, circuit lengths, water temperature, floor finish and room-by-room demand influence the final layout and operating response.

planningProcess.title: Every circuit begins with a considered decision before the floor is covered.
planningProcess.intro: Whether the underfloor network already exists or is included in GOLD Group's scope, review begins with the building plans, calculated load, floor build-up and control zones so the distribution network can be coordinated with the heat source.
planningProcess.imageAlt: Illustrative hydronic underfloor-heating loop plan connected to a central manifold

steps.title: Study the inputs
steps.body: Review room loads, insulation, floor construction, manifold position and required control zones.

steps.title: Plan the circuits
steps.body: Coordinate pipe spacing, loop length, routing and edge zones for each space.

steps.title: Install and verify
steps.body: Coordinate connections, pressure-test the circuits and document their routes before completing the floor build-up.

applications.title: Heat source
applications.body: A correctly selected heat pump can supply the low-temperature water commonly used by hydronic floor circuits.

applications.title: Distribution
applications.body: Manifolds divide the system into balanced loops with suitable flow rates and circuit lengths.

applications.title: Room control
applications.body: Zoning, thermostats and actuators coordinate room demand without losing sight of minimum flow and heat-source operation.

underfloorHeating.seriesBody: Underfloor heating can be a strong match for an air-to-water heat pump because it generally works with lower supply-water temperatures than many traditional emitters. The heat pump, buffer strategy, circulation and controls still need to be selected around the actual project.

series.title: Steady water temperature
series.body: Weather compensation and suitable controls can adjust supply temperature as outdoor conditions change.

series.title: Coordinated circulation
series.body: Pump selection, manifold pressure loss and zone operation must maintain the intended flow through active circuits.

underfloorHeating.factorsTitle: Design inputs that shape the floor system
underfloorHeating.factorsIntro: The final design should follow calculated room loads and the actual floor construction rather than a single standard spacing rule.

factors.title: Building heat load
factors.body: Room-by-room demand defines the heat output required from each floor area.

factors.title: Insulation
factors.body: Building-envelope and below-pipe insulation influence losses, response and required output.

factors.title: Pipe spacing and layout
factors.body: Spacing, loop pattern, circuit length and edge zones respond to room demand and geometry.

factors.title: Floor construction
factors.body: Screed depth, floor finish and material resistance affect heat transfer and response time.

factors.title: Supply-water temperature
factors.body: The design temperature must suit the calculated output, floor limits and selected heat source.

factors.title: Zoning and controls
factors.body: Manifolds, actuators, thermostats, sensors and circulation logic coordinate individual areas.

underfloorHeating.closingTitle: Begin with the building plan and heating load.
underfloorHeating.closingBody: Share the project location, floor plans, construction, insulation and intended heat source so the system direction can be reviewed before equipment and pipe quantities are finalized.

contact.title: Start with the building. We will help frame the system.
contact.intro: Tell us the building type, area, location, required water temperature and intended heating or cooling distribution. These details help us begin with the right questions.

footer.summary: Heat-pump, heating, cooling and hot-water system solutions across all Syrian governorates.
